preamble: The phonetic transformation from the diphthong /ue/ to /uo/ involves a backing and rounding of the second vowel element, where the mid-front unrounded vowel /e/ shifts to a mid-back rounded vowel /o/. This type of vocalic change can occur in specific morphophonological contexts.
phoneticEffects: Backing of the second vowel element, Rounding of the second vowel element

examples.note: This vowel change represents an "undoing" of morphophonological ablaut found in present stems, where 'duoda' is the predicted infinitive based on the present stem 'duedu'.

sources: Good, J. C. (2005). Strong linearity: Three case studies towards a theory of morphosyntactic templatic constructions. University at Buffalo.
